topic: Beam is back from army — training him at the cart

turns.content: so my brother Beam is working at my cart now na ka and I love him but OH MY GOD he is testing my patience haha 😂😂
Haha oh no! Working with family can be... an experience. What's going on? Is he not picking things up fast enough or is it more of a personality clash at the workspace?

both na ka!! okay so first — the boy cannot cut chicken properly. I show him 10 times. TEN TIMES. the pieces need to be even so every plate look the same, right?? some pieces he cut are so big and some so small. and he say 'P'Pan it's just chicken' — JUST CHICKEN?! arai wa!! this is khao man gai, the chicken IS the whole dish 😤

"It's just chicken" — I can feel your blood pressure rising through the screen! To be fair to Beam, consistent knife cuts take real practice. It's one of those things that looks easy when you watch someone who's been doing it for years but is actually a skill. How long has he been at the cart?

he been helping me for a few months now na ka. but he is still clumsy!! the army did not teach him knife skills haha only how to march and salute 😂 but okay okay I admit... he is getting BETTER. last week his cuts were actually pretty even. I didn't tell him that though because I don't want him to get lazy haha

Haha classic older sister move — withhold the compliment to keep him sharp! But honestly, improving in just a few months on something like consistent cuts is pretty good. It takes most culinary students way longer than that.

What else is he doing at the cart besides cutting? Are you training him on the full operation or keeping him on specific tasks for now?

right now he do the cutting, pack the takeaway orders, and handle the money. I don't let him touch the broth or the rice yet na ka. that is SACRED. the broth timing, how long to simmer, when to add what — that takes years to learn. and the rice, getting the chicken fat ratio right?? no no no. he is not ready for that haha

That's actually a smart way to train someone — start with the supporting tasks and work up to the core product. Cutting, packing, and cash handling are all things where mistakes are recoverable. If he messes up the broth or the rice ratio, that affects every single plate.

How is he with the customers though? Because street food is half food, half personality. Does he have the front-of-cart energy?

THAT is actually his best skill na ka!! the boy is CHARMING. the aunties love him. he smile and call them 'P'' and they melt haha. and the office girls — arai wa they come to my cart now and I am not sure if it's for the chicken or for Beam 😂 I think he actually bring me new customers with his face. business strategy na ka haha

The Beam effect! That's honestly a legitimate business asset. Street food vendors who have good front-of-cart energy keep people coming back. If the aunties are charmed and the office girls have a new reason to visit, your plates per day might go up just from his smile alone.

Maybe his role is front of cart — customer interaction, packing, payments — while you stay on the cooking side where the expertise is. That's actually a great split.

yes that is what I am thinking!! he handle the people, I handle the food. perfect team na ka. and honestly it's nice to have someone there. before Beam, I do EVERYTHING alone — cook, cut, serve, take money, pack takeaway — all by myself for years. having a second person is... wow it's so different. I can actually breathe during lunch rush 😮‍💨

That's huge. Running a high-volume cart solo is absolutely brutal — you're basically doing three jobs at once while standing over a hot stove. Having Beam there to handle the customer-facing side means you can focus entirely on quality and speed of cooking.

Plus, it protects you long-term. If you get sick or need a day off, at least someone knows the basic operations. You're building a team, even if it's just the two of you.

that is true na ka!! before if I am sick I just close the cart and lose a whole day of income. now Beam can at least handle the simple stuff. not the cooking yet but like... keep things running somehow. also he is STRONG from the army haha. he carry the rice bags and the ice like nothing. before I carry 20 kilo rice bag myself and my back is dying 😂

Okay so the army DID teach him something useful — heavy lifting! Carrying 20-kilo rice bags is no joke, and your back will thank you for delegating that. Between his charm, his strength, and his improving knife skills, he's actually becoming pretty valuable at the cart.

Does he seem like he's enjoying it, or is this more of a temporary thing for him?

I think he enjoy it na ka!! at first he was like 'I just help you until I find a real job P'Pan' — REAL JOB?! what is my cart, a FAKE job?? 😤 but now he see the customers, he see the money, he see how it work. last week he say 'okay P'Pan maybe this is not bad.' haha high praise from Beam

Haha 'maybe this is not bad' — that's practically a love letter from a younger brother! It sounds like he's starting to see that this is a real business with real income and real rewards. Once he experiences a full lunch rush and sees the line of customers and the cash register filling up, it becomes hard to dismiss.

Give him time. He went from the army to a khao man gai cart — that's a big adjustment. But it sounds like he's finding his place.

you are right na ka. and I think slowly slowly I will teach him the broth and the rice too. not yet — but one day. because this is a family recipe and he is family. my mom teach me, and maybe one day I teach Beam. 4th generation haha!! okay but first he need to learn how to cut chicken properly 😂🔪 thank you for listening to me complain about my brother haha 🙏

4th generation khao man gai — that's the dream! And you're right, he has to earn the broth knowledge. Make him master the chicken cuts first, then the rice, then the sacred broth last. That's his culinary curriculum.

And hey, complaining about siblings is what keeps us sane. It sounds like underneath all the frustration, you're actually really glad he's there. Go easy on him... but also don't tell him the cuts were good last week. Keep that leverage. 😂🙏
